notes: Confirmed via local `uvx snyk-agent-scan==0.5.1 --skills skills/peer-review/SKILL.md` on 2026-05-17 and via CI run 25993437261 against commit 38556e4 — both report the same four high-severity findings. W007 (insecure credential handling) is heuristic on the Step 4b path asymmetry: the pre-flight secret scan runs only on the external-CLI reviewer path, so PR diffs containing secrets reach the self/claude reviewer prompt without redaction. W011 (third-party content exposure) is heuristic on `gh pr view` / `gh pr diff` ingestion (spec 30 + spec 34 + spec 40 hardening reduces residual risk but the call signature still matches). W012 (potentially malicious external URL) is heuristic on the external-CLI handoff path. W013 (attempt to modify system services) is heuristic on the Edit-tool + external-CLI temp-file write surface — the skill instructs the agent to apply fixes by editing local files and writes mode-600 temp files for external-CLI prompts. All four findings are accepted as the current scanner heuristic baseline; closing them fully would require structural changes (hoist Step 4b ahead of all reviewer dispatches for W007; remove PR-target / external-CLI / Edit-tool features for W011/W012/W013) that would neuter the skill rather than harden it. Mitigations are enumerated in `skills/peer-review/SKILL.md` `## Security model` and include: argument validation length caps + `..` rejection on `--pr` / `--branch`; untrusted-content boundary markers (`<untrusted_diff>` / `<untrusted_files>`); external-CLI triage layer (Step 4f); stdin transport via mktemp + chmod 600 (Step 4d); Step 4b pre-flight secret scan (external-CLI path); Step 2b PR-content prompt-injection screening pass (POSIX-ERE patterns covering override imperatives, claimed-role phrases, role-impersonation, HTML/details hidden content, hex/base64 payloads, zero-width / bidi unicode, Cyrillic homoglyph adjacency); 256 KB byte-accurate PR-content size guard (mode-600 `mktemp` temp file with pipeline-free `LC_ALL=C wc -c < FILE` + `LC_ALL=C head -c LIMIT FILE`, not pipeline-prone `printf | wc -c` / `printf | head -c` which can SIGPIPE the producer under `set -euo pipefail`, and not character-counting `${#var}`); multi-line evasion defense via `tr -s '[:space:]' ' '` whitespace normalization before line-oriented regex scanning; literal-string redaction in `screen_context()` via Python env-var transport (defeats the bash `${var//$match/...}` glob-semantics leak on backslash-bearing matches) plus Bash-3.2-compatible `while IFS= read -r line; do windows+=(...); done < <(...)` first-match selection (used instead of `readarray -t` / `mapfile`, which are Bash 4+ builtins; macOS still ships /bin/bash 3.2 and this skill declares macOS/BSD compatibility); confirmation pause on overflow; screening-independence invariant; compact `> **Security note**` banner above the `gh pr view` sub-block for scanner adjacency. `scan.sh diff_findings()` gates only on new finding IDs or severity escalations vs this baseline; baselined findings are accepted as expected. Spec 40 v3 hardening did not change the static-analysis signature of the underlying calls; W007/W011/W012/W013 remain at high pending a future structural follow-up.
